steps.title: Read the igneous frequency from the table
steps.content: Looking at the frequency table: igneous rocks have a frequency of 10. The problem states there are 70 rocks total in the collection.

steps.title: Apply the probability formula
steps.content:
Probability of selecting a rock is the ratio of favorable outcomes to total outcomes:
$$P(\text{igneous}) = \frac{\text{Number of igneous rocks}}{\text{Total number of rocks}} = \frac{10}{70}$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
